title: Peppery Hangover Malaysian Ginger Soup
description: A spicy, comforting soup featuring ground ginger to cure those hangover blues, all in a rich and flavorful Malaysian style.
instructions: Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the sliced ginger and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk, chicken broth, soy sauce, and 1 teaspoon of black pepper. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the soup simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the beaten eggs. Cook for another 2 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked through.
Season the soup with additional black pepper to taste. Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of black pepper and a squeeze of fresh lime juice.
To make the soup more substantial, serve with some steamed rice or noodles on the side.
For an extra kick, add a dash of hot sauce, such as sriracha, to the soup.
Refrigerate or freeze the soup for up to 3 days for a quick and easy breakfast or snack.
For a creamier soup, use full-fat coconut milk and add a tablespoon of cornstarch to thicken the soup.
To make the soup more filling, add some diced chicken or tofu for extra protein.
Experiment with different spices, such as cumin or coriander, to add more depth to the soup.
